label: star-like motes — multi-stage bloom + warm-white tint
notes: Replaced the single-pass bloom with a 3-stage Gaussian stack chained through 2-input compositeTOPs (lesson from move_008's failed 3-input attempt: compositeTOP only has 2 input slots; chain them). Stack: render → blur_inner(size=4) → level_inner(brightness=16) | render → blur_motes(size=35, preshrink=2) → level_bloom(brightness=2.5) | render → blur_outer(size=60, preshrink=4) → level_outer(brightness=1.0). Composites: comp_bloom = render+inner; comp_b = comp_bloom+mid; comp_c = comp_b+outer; null_motes_out → comp_c. Sphere shrunk to 0.008 (essentially a point — bloom carries the form). Material tinted warm-white (1.0, 0.95, 0.78). Tried anisotropic blur (filterscalex=8, size=90) for cross-flare star spikes — didn't produce sharp diffraction-spike rays (just stretched gaussians, invisible & expensive at 12 fps), reverted. True IT-cistern diffraction spikes would need a custom GLSL star-filter shader.
